<style>
  .profile-pic {
    float: right;
    width: 175px; /* Adjust size as needed */
    height: 175px;
    margin-left: 15px;
    margin-bottom: 10px;
    border-radius: 40%;
    object-fit: cover;
    /* This makes the text follow the curve of the circle */
    shape-outside: circle(50%);
  }
</style>

# Who am I?
<img src="profile.jpg" class="profile-pic">
I'm Santiago Zúñiga, a Mechanical Engineer from Argentina with a Ph.D. focused on Computational Fluid Dynamics (CFD). My work revolves around understanding complex fluid behaviors, exploring everything from multi-scale multiphase flows and thermal analysis to fluid-structure interactions. I regularly work with high-performance computing clusters to run DNS, LES, and RANS simulations.

Beyond the computer screen, I also enjoy hands-on experimental work. I have practical experience in the lab conducting wind tunnel tests, hot-wire anemometry, and PIV measurements. Additionally, I am passionate about teaching. I love sharing my enthusiasm for fluid mechanics and helping students bridge the gap between complex theory and practical engineering.

Feel free to explore the links above to see my latest projects! If this interests you, let's connect!

{{< iconify tabler brand-linkedin >}} [LinkedIn](https://www.linkedin.com/in/zunigasantiago/) | {{< iconify tabler mail >}} [Email Me](mailto:santiago.zuniga@ib.edu.ar)
